pi_items.verified_value: PhysioNet EEGMMI dataset, S001R01.edf (eyes open baseline, 1,275,936 bytes, 64 channels at 160 Hz)

pi_items.metadata_value: PhysioNet Credentialed Health Data License — publicly available
pi_items.verified_value: Open Data Commons Attribution License v1.0 (publicly available)
pi_items.source: https://www.physionet.org/content/eegmmidb/1.0.0/ - shows license as ODC-BY-1.0, not PhysioNet Credentialed License

pi_items.verified_value: 5 s (available option in Time Series widget dropdown)
pi_items.source: Task description specifies '5 seconds (select "5 s")'. OpenBCI GUI documentation confirms Time Series widget has window duration dropdown options.

pi_items.verified_value: 50 uV (available option in Time Series widget)
pi_items.source: Task description specifies '50 µV (select "50 uV")'. OpenBCI GUI documentation confirms vertical scale control in microvolts.
Task description specifies PLAYBACK mode, setup scripts confirm playback file operations
PhysioNet EEGMMI documentation confirms 64-channel EEG recordings: https://www.physionet.org/content/eegmmidb/1.0.0/

pi_items.verified_value: 160 Hz (EEGMMI original) → resampled to 250 Hz for OpenBCI format
pi_items.source: PhysioNet EEGMMI: 160 Hz. Install script converts to 250 Hz via scipy.signal.resample()

privileged_info_summary: The task uses OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 to configure Time Series widget settings (5-second window, 50 µV vertical scale) for playback of real EEG data from PhysioNet's publicly-available EEGMMI dataset (Subject 001, Run 01 - eyes open baseline, S001R01.edf). The dataset contains 64-channel EEG at 160 Hz (resampled to 250 Hz for playback). The install script incorrectly identifies the license as 'PhysioNet Credentialed Health Data License' when the actual license is the 'Open Data Commons Attribution License v1.0'.
audit_notes: CRITICAL ISSUE: The task.json file is corrupted and incomplete (952 bytes, cuts off mid-string at line 5). The 'metadata' field is not present in the available file. All verified claims above come from task description, setup scripts, and external sources. License claim in install script contradicts official PhysioNet repository which lists ODC-BY-1.0 as the license.
